mappings.rationale: Directly covers the major planets of the solar system, their surfaces, atmospheres, and satellites, which are the primary focus of this section.
Covers the solar system as a planetary system, theories of origin, and the regularities and interactions among solar system bodies.
Directly covers the Sun's surface layers, corona, chromosphere, sunspots, solar cycle, solar radiation, and solar-terrestrial relationships discussed in this section.
Directly covers the Moon, its physical nature, surface features, origin and evolution, and the satellites of other planets discussed in this section.
Covers the Earth as a planet including its orbital motion, physical properties, magnetism, and internal structure discussed in this section.
Relevant to theories of the origin of the solar system and the cosmological context for planetary system formation.
Relevant to the solar system's place within the Milky Way Galaxy and the broader cosmic context.
Relevant to the Sun as a star, solar energy generation, stellar evolution, and the formation of planetary systems around stars.
Covers the physical processes governing the Sun and solar system, relevant to solar interior physics and energy generation.
Covers the historical development of our understanding of the solar system including planetary observations and theories of origin.
Covers planetary surfaces, atmospheres, and conditions relevant to the habitability of solar system bodies discussed in this section.
Relevant to gravitational interactions among solar system bodies, orbital mechanics, and the astronomical unit discussed in this section.
Covers the observational instruments used to study the Sun, planets, moons, and other solar system bodies.
Relevant to meteorites and the dating of the solar system's age through geological and radiometric evidence.
Relevant to the Earth as a planet, its internal structure, surface features, and the geological processes shaping terrestrial planets.
Covers the Earth's magnetism, temperature, physical properties, and internal structure discussed in this section.
Relevant to the structure and composition of the Earth's interior and the geological processes on terrestrial planets.

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationale: Directly relevant to the Sun-Earth-Moon system, gravitational interactions, and tidal phenomena discussed in this section.
Relevant to the astronomical basis of chronology, the day, month, year, and the Sun-Earth-Moon system as discussed in this section.
Relevant to radiometric dating methods used to determine the ages of meteorites and the solar system.
Relevant to the Earth's magnetism and the Sun's magnetic fields including centres of activity and the sunspot cycle.
Relevant to planetary atmospheres and the Earth's atmosphere as discussed in the context of the Earth as a planet.
Relevant to the historical understanding of the solar system's structure and the Earth's orbital motion around the Sun.
Relevant to the early telescopic observations of planets, moons, and the Sun that advanced understanding of the solar system.
Relevant to gravitational theory governing planetary orbits and the mechanics of the solar system discussed in this section.
Relevant to solar-terrestrial relationships and the effects of solar radiation on Earth's climate discussed in this section.
Relevant to surface features of terrestrial planets and the geological processes shaping planetary surfaces.
Relevant to the chemical composition and analysis of lunar rock samples and meteorites discussed in this section.
Relevant to the composition and physical properties of lunar rocks and meteorites discussed in this section.
Relevant to solar radiation, aberration of light, and the observational methods used to study the solar system.
Relevant to the astronomical basis of navigation using the Sun, Moon, and stars, and the motions of the Sun-Earth-Moon system.
Relevant to surface features of Mars and other terrestrial planets that share desert-like conditions discussed in this section.
Relevant to isotopic analysis of lunar samples and meteorites used to determine the ages and origins of solar system materials.
Relevant to the effects of Earth's orbital position on climate and the astronomical basis of glacial cycles.
